Agent1 Round 1: The pterygomandibular space lies deep within the infratemporal fossa between the ramus of mandible and levator palpebrae superioris. It is bounded by several important structures including nerves that supply muscles involved in mastication as well as blood vessels. 

The correct option is D: Nerve to medial pterygoid muscle - This nerve typically courses through the masseteric (not pterygomandibular) space supplying the medial pterygoid muscle. A thorough understanding of anatomical spaces ensures accurate diagnosis and management of pathologies affecting these regions. Clinical Reasoning Completed.
Answer: D

history.message:
Agent2 Round 2: I concur that option D, "Nerve to the medial pterygoid muscle," correctly identifies something not contained in the pterygomandibular space. One additional point supporting this exclusion is that the nerve to the medial pterygoid originates from the deep surface of the parotid gland and travels along the masseter muscle before entering the substance of the medial pterygoid muscle itself. This route contrasts distinctly with the course taken by other nerves mentioned—such as the long buccal nerve—which indeed traverse parts of the pterygomandibular space en route to supplying adjacent mucosa.
Answer: D
